moves.note: Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety
Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the 20% single name cap.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and lower volatility, aligning with conservative goals.
Buy XLF at 5% to diversify into a strong, non-tech sector setup.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration and manage risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Expect slower growth but enhanced stability.

moves.note: Eliminate leveraged ETF with stop hit
Add to strong performing AI name
Add quality tech name from scanner

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single name concentration limit.
Sell SOXL completely as it is below its stop, reducing risk.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce overall leveraged ETF exposure.
Add to CRWD, increasing its allocation to 18% to lean into a strong performer.
Buy PANW at 5% and MSFT at 5%, adding high-quality tech setups from the scanner.
Maintain a balanced approach, blending broad index with quality tech and managed leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and diversifies into strong tech setups. Expect moderate growth with controlled drawdown.

moves.note: Exit below stop, reallocate to strong

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to adhere to the single name concentration limit.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop, reallocating capital to stronger setups.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector.
Add to CRWD, increasing its allocation to 25% to press a strong uptrending AI name.
Buy NVDU at 10% and PANW at 10%, adding high-scoring, high-conviction setups from the scanner.
This plan leans into leverage and concentration for aggressive growth, maintaining strict stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month, aiming for stretch growth by pressing strong setups and using leverage. Be aware of significantly higher drawdown potential and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and enhance stability for income generation.
Increase QQQI to 35%, significantly boosting exposure to income-focused ETFs for monthly distributions.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing low drawdown.
Trim CRWD to 5% and SMH to 5% to reduce high-beta single name and sector exposure.
This strategy aims to park cash for yield, focusing on steady distributions over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for cash-like income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in a selloff. This is not a savings account.
